title: Van Gend en Loos
text:
Title: Van Gend en Loos
Citation: Case 26/62
Jurisdiction: eu
Source URL: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/

The Court of Justice held that EU law can confer rights on individuals enforceable in national courts. The case established direct effect and described the Community as a new legal order of international law.

title: Costa v ENEL
text:
Title: Costa v ENEL
Citation: Case 6/64
Jurisdiction: eu
Source URL: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/

The Court of Justice held that EU law has primacy over conflicting national law. Costa v ENEL is foundational for supremacy, uniformity, and the autonomous legal order of the European Union.

title: Cassis de Dijon
text:
Title: Cassis de Dijon
Citation: Case 120/78
Jurisdiction: eu
Source URL: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/

The Court held that goods lawfully marketed in one Member State should generally be admitted in others unless justified by mandatory requirements. The case created mutual recognition and proportionality analysis in free movement of goods.

title: Francovich
text:
Title: Francovich
Citation: Joined Cases C-6/90 and C-9/90
Jurisdiction: eu
Source URL: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/

The Court recognized Member State liability in damages for failure to implement EU directives where the directive confers rights, content is identifiable, and causation exists. Francovich is central to effective judicial protection and enforcement of EU law.

title: Kadi
citation: Joined Cases C-402/05 P and C-415/05 P
text:
Title: Kadi
Citation: Joined Cases C-402/05 P and C-415/05 P
Jurisdiction: eu
Source URL: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/

The Court reviewed EU measures implementing UN sanctions against EU fundamental rights standards. Kadi is important for autonomy of EU law, judicial protection, due process, and interaction between EU law and international security obligations.

title: Data Protection Commissioner v Facebook Ireland and Schrems
source_name: Data Protection Commissioner v Facebook Ireland and Schrems
text:
Title: Data Protection Commissioner v Facebook Ireland and Schrems
Citation: Case C-311/18, Schrems II
Jurisdiction: eu
Source URL: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/

The Court invalidated the EU-US Privacy Shield adequacy decision and upheld standard contractual clauses subject to effective protection assessment. Schrems II is central to international data transfers, surveillance concerns, and GDPR enforcement.
